signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBA Pause
signals.text: USD/AUD exhibits a 1.566% G8 spread as USD outperforms (+0.507%) while AUD lags (-1.060%) following RBA minutes signaling a potential pause after three hikes. Despite a -60bp AUD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), the divergence is driven by the shift in RBA bias. USD/AUD at 0.7119 remains under pressure as the G8 spread exceeds the 1.20% critical threshold.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs short) vs retail 86% long (extreme retail long) at 0.5843. This institutional vs retail divergence occurs ahead of the 27 May RBNZ meeting where a hike bias remains. Retail is aggressively fading the institutional short; capitulation risk is elevated if price breaks lower, while a hawkish RBNZ delivery triggers a crowded spec short unwind.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.6% generates an elevated +3.4% Vol Risk Premium, signaling intense hedging demand. COT LF net long +40,980 (70% long) shows crowded institutional positioning while 25d RR at -1.20 indicates the options market is paying for downside protection. Market is heavily long but increasingly fearful of a reversal following weak UK jobs data.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.08 — 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. 25d RR at -1.66 (strong put skew) confirms the options market is aggressively hedging for JPY strength/USD weakness despite the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. Speculative positioning remains heavily short (LF net -70,605), leaving the pair vulnerable to a rapid unwind if 160.00 is tested.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Carry + Inflation Catalyst
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3760 is supported by a +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%). COT LF net short -39,960 (28.4% long) shows specs are positioned for CAD weakness. Today's Canada CPI release is the primary catalyst; a higher-than-expected print is required to challenge the current carry-driven upside and shift the BoC hold bias.
